findings.message: The new POST handler routes its data access through `..db.item_service` (add_item), a second data-access module that duplicates the existing `..db.repository`. repository.py already exposes a `create_item(session, name)` that performs the same insert AND owns the commit/refresh. The codebase's established seam is routes -> repository (see get_items calling list_items). Introducing item_service as a parallel persistence path is a duplicate data-access layer and inappropriate cross-module coupling: two modules now own item persistence, so callers must know which to use and transaction semantics diverge between them.
findings.suggestion: Drop the item_service import and call the existing `repository.create_item(session, payload.name)`, which already commits/refreshes. Consolidate on a single repository module so persistence has one owner; if item_service is meant to replace repository, migrate get_items too and delete the duplicate rather than running both.

findings.message: Transaction management leaks into the route layer: create_item calls `session.commit()` / `session.refresh()` inline because the chosen helper `add_item` only flushes. The sibling repository.create_item encapsulates commit/refresh behind the data-access boundary (as get_items/list_items do for reads). Having the HTTP handler drive the commit couples the route to persistence/transaction concerns the data-access layer is supposed to hide, and is inconsistent with the read path in the same file.
findings.suggestion: Delegate the full unit of work (insert + commit + refresh) to a single repository function (repository.create_item) and have the handler simply return its result, keeping transaction boundaries inside the data-access layer.

findings.message: `fetch_all_items` is imported from the duplicate item_service module but never used in the diff, signalling the parallel data-access module is being wired in speculatively (further coupling the route file to the redundant layer).
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused `fetch_all_items` import; reads already go through repository.list_items.
